. Flowcart does this at the supply and distribution layer; Bumpa does it at the point of sale," Jobtech Alliance said. Flowcart is an AI-powered conversational commerce and CRM platform built on WhatsApp, while Bumpa simplifies business operations with tools that make inventory, sales, orders and customer management easy.

Contenu:
Titre:
SA's $21.4m University Technology Fund II to invest in university-originated innovations


Résumé:
Stocks & Strauss Fund Manager has launched University Technology Fund II (UTF II), a ZAR400 million (US$21.4 million) fund targeting 15-20 university-originated deep-tech startups. Building on UTF I's successes like Hyrax Biosciences and CubeSpace, UTF II expands its mandate to include alumni of South African universities. Anchor investors include SA SME Fund, Stellenbosch University, Allan & Gill Gray Philanthropies Africa, University of Pretoria, University of Cape Town, and WITS University.

text:
Titre:
SA's $21.4m University Technology Fund II to invest in university-originated innovations


Date:
2025-01-30


Catégorie:
funding


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2025/01/30/sas-21-4m-university-technology-fund-ii-to-invest-in-university-originated-innovations


Contenu:
Stocks & Strauss Fund Manager has announced the University Technology Fund II (UTF II), which achieved a major milestone in December 2024 with all agreements concluded and a substantial portion of the funds committed, marking a significant step towards reaching the ZAR400 million (US$21.4 million) target fund size. UTF I demonstrated the immense potential of university-originated technologies. Among its notable investments were Hyrax Biosciences, a leader in bioinformatics, whose software facilitated the early detection and understanding of the Covid-19 Omicron variant, and CubeSpace, a globally acclaimed company recognised by NASA and trusted by more than 250 global customers for its advanced satellite control system and related components. UTF II aims to invest in 15 to 20 companies, focusing on highly scalable technology IP across various sectors. The fund will channel resources into technology commercialisation and growth, ensuring a targeted approach to supporting innovative ventures with high potential. In an effort to strengthen the country's ecosystem of entrepreneurs, UTF II has expanded its mandate to allocate a portion of the fund to include alumni of South African universities. This approach aims to create a more interconnected ecosystem around universities, similar to the role Stanford University played in fostering Silicon Valley

metadata.title: SA's $21.4m University Technology Fund II to invest in university-originated innovations

text:
Titre:
SA's $21.4m University Technology Fund II to invest in university-originated innovations


Date:
2025-01-30


Catégorie:
funding


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2025/01/30/sas-21-4m-university-technology-fund-ii-to-invest-in-university-originated-innovations


Contenu:
. This approach aims to create a more interconnected ecosystem around universities, similar to the role Stanford University played in fostering Silicon Valley. The fund's anchor investors include the SA SME Fund, Stellenbosch University, and Allan & Gill Gray Philanthropies Africa, reinforcing a co-investment model based on collaboration and shared vision. Other key university investors in UTF II include the University of Pretoria, the University of Cape Town, and WITS University, further strengthening the initiative's commitment to fostering innovation. "The potential is immense, both for the companies and for investors. The world is witnessing a shift towards valuing university intellectual property as an important asset class and opportunity. These companies are also providing solutions to real-world challenges, which boosts their impact and value. If you're seeking deep-tech investment opportunities in Africa, universities are unparalleled hubs. They possess the capacity, skilled talent, cross-faculty institutional knowledge, and long-term funding necessary to drive meaningful innovation and development in this space," said Wayne Stocks, managing partner at Stocks & Strauss Fund Manager. Anita Nel, chief director for innovation and commercialisation at Stellenbosch University, said her university had partnered with the University Technology Fund II to support innovation on its campus and to help students, staff, and alumni bring their ideas to market

metadata.title: SA's $21.4m University Technology Fund II to invest in university-originated innovations

SA's $21.4m University Technology Fund II to invest in university-originated innovations

Contenu:
. Anita Nel, chief director for innovation and commercialisation at Stellenbosch University, said her university had partnered with the University Technology Fund II to support innovation on its campus and to help students, staff, and alumni bring their ideas to market. "The fund accelerates the development of new technologies and reinforces the university's reputation as a hub for research and innovation and builds on the successful relationship between our university and the UTF I. Additionally, the partnership offers potential financial returns that can be reinvested in research and infrastructure," she said. Ketso Gordhan, CEO of the SA SME Fund, emphasised the importance of UTF's role in shaping South Africa's economic future. "UTF Fund I is a powerful example of what can be achieved when we align resources to unlock the potential of South Africa's brightest minds and world class intellectual property. UTF Fund II will elevate our innovation landscape further and we foresee that it will attract both local and international investment interest," he said.

Catalyst Fund marks 1st close of target $40m fund to propel African climate innovation


Résumé:
Catalyst Fund, a pre-seed venture capital fund and accelerator focused on climate resilience innovation in Africa, announced the first close of its targeted $40 million fund with over 20% committed. Backers include FSD Africa, FSDAi, Cisco Foundation, USAID Prosper Africa, and Andrew Bredenkamp. The fund will support 40 pre-seed startups in agri-tech, fishery management, food systems, insurtech, climate fintech, cold chain, waste management, and water management, with a focus on local and women founders, providing capital and hands-on venture building support.

Contenu:
Catalyst Fund, a pre-seed venture capital fund and accelerator focused on driving climate resilience innovation in Africa, has announced the successful first close of its targeted US$40 million fund, with over 20 per cent committed. Catalyst Fund has garnered significant backing from investors including FSD Africa, FSDAi, Cisco Foundation, USAID Prosper Africa, and seasoned tech investor Andrew Bredenkamp, all of whom share its mission to enhance the reach and impact of pre-seed companies at the forefront of building a climate-resilient future in Africa. With support from JPMorgan Chase & Co and the Global Environmental Facility, Catalyst Fund also works to provide dedicated impact measurement and research support to climate innovators, share insights and learnings, and enable partnerships via its Ecosystem Hub. With a strategic focus on solutions in agri-tech and fishery management, food systems, insurtech and climate fintech, cold chain, waste management, and water management, Catalyst Fund is expediting sustainable green growth. It will focus on solutions that can enable communities to better prepare for and manage shocks, adapt livelihoods to climate impacts and build long-term resilience. The fund will support 40 pre-seed startups, with a focus on backing mission-driven local and women founders, offering essential early-stage capital and pairing it with hands-on, tailor-made venture building support to accelerate startups' growth

The fund will support 40 pre-seed startups, with a focus on backing mission-driven local and women founders, offering essential early-stage capital and pairing it with hands-on, tailor-made venture building support to accelerate startups' growth. The fund's commitment extends beyond the initial stage, as it pledges to provide follow-on capital to startups in their seed and Series A rounds. By bridging this critical funding gap, the Catalyst Fund addresses a key bottleneck for climate ventures in Africa, positioning them for further growth-stage funding at Series B. "By blending equity investments with hands-on venture building, we believe we can unlock tremendous potential for innovative companies on the continent. Supporting ventures at the pre-seed stage requires more than capital. Our venture builders are the engineers, data scientists and growth marketing experts who can supercharge founders' journeys toward building scalable and highly impactful ventures," said Maelis Carraro, managing partner of the Catalyst Fund. "At FSDAi we believe strongly that Africa has the potential to lead in climate solutions provided it has the finance it needs. Our mission is to support that by investing in innovative solutions that will help channel finance to under-resourced parts of the green economy

Le Conseil du deuxième district examine les projets bloqués et accélère le programme de développement 2025


Date:

Catégorie:

Date:

Secteurs:



Pays:
Tunisia


Startups mentionnées:



Investisseurs:

Article:
Le Conseil du deuxième district, qui regroupe les gouvernorats de Tunis, Ben Arous, Ariana, La Manouba, Nabeul et Zaghouan, a tenu dimanche une réunion de travail avec le conseil régional et les conseils locaux du gouvernorat de Tunis afin d'examiner l'état d'avancement des projets bloqués et d'accélérer la mise en œuvre du programme de développement intégré pour 2025. La réunion, organisée au siège de la municipalité de La Kasbah à Tunis, s'inscrit dans le cadre du renforcement de la coordination entre les différentes structures des pouvoirs local et régional. Les participants ont passé en revue les projets en souffrance, identifié les principaux obstacles à leur réalisation et souligné la nécessité d'accélérer leur exécution afin d'améliorer l'efficacité des interventions de développement et de répondre aux attentes des citoyens. Les discussions ont également insisté sur l'importance d'un suivi régulier des projets et d'une meilleure coordination entre les différentes parties prenantes. Les conclusions, recommandations et décisions issues de cette réunion seront annoncées après l'achèvement des travaux des commissions techniques et la consolidation de leurs propositions.

metadata.title: Le Conseil du deuxième district examine les projets bloqués et accélère le programme de développement 2025

Titre:
Forte hausse des prix des brebis et recul de la production de viande rouge entre 2010 et 2026


Date:
2026-07-06


Catégorie:
news


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://africanmanager.com/forte-hausse-des-prix-des-brebis-et-recul-de-la-production-de-viande-rouge-entre-2010-et-2026


Contenu:
Titre:
Forte hausse des prix des brebis et recul de la production de viande rouge entre 2010 et 2026


Résumé:
Les prix des brebis en Tunisie ont été multipliés par plus de huit entre 2010 et 2026, passant de 240-300 dinars à près de 2 500 dinars, soit une hausse d'environ 800 %. Parallèlement, la production nationale de viande rouge a reculé d'environ 10 % sur la même période, passant de 122 700 tonnes en 2010 à 110 800 tonnes en 2025. Cette évolution s'explique par l'envolée du coût des aliments pour bétail, les sécheresses successives, la diminution du cheptel reproducteur, la baisse du nombre d'éleveurs et les difficultés d'accès au financement. Le ministère de l'Agriculture a présenté un programme de relance à l'horizon 2030 pour reconstituer le cheptel et améliorer la productivité de la filière.

text:
Titre:
Forte hausse des prix des brebis et recul de la production de viande rouge entre 2010 et 2026


Date:
2026-07-06


Catégorie:
news


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://africanmanager.com/forte-hausse-des-prix-des-brebis-et-recul-de-la-production-de-viande-rouge-entre-2010-et-2026


Contenu:
La hausse spectaculaire des prix du cheptel en Tunisie, où le prix moyen d'une brebis a été multiplié par plus de huit en seize ans, illustre les difficultés structurelles d'une filière fragilisée par le recul de la production, la diminution du cheptel reproducteur, l'augmentation des coûts de production et les effets des sécheresses successives, selon des données officielles. D'après les statistiques de l'Office de l'élevage et des pâturages (OEP), le prix moyen d'une brebis est passé d'une fourchette comprise entre 240 et 300 dinars en 2010 à près de 2 500 dinars au cours du premier semestre 2026, soit une hausse d'environ 800 %. Parallèlement, la production nationale de viande rouge a reculé d'environ 10 % sur la même période, passant de 122 700 tonnes en 2010 à quelque 110 800 tonnes en 2025, alors que la demande demeure soutenue. Les données présentées le 2 juillet devant la commission des secteurs productifs du Conseil national des régions et des districts (CNRD) attribuent cette évolution à une combinaison de facteurs économiques et climatiques. Les représentants du ministère de l'Agriculture évoquent notamment l'envolée du coût des aliments pour bétail, sous l'effet de la hausse des prix mondiaux des céréales, du renchérissement des importations et du transport, ainsi que des épisodes répétés de sécheresse qui ont réduit la production de fourrages

metadata.title: Forte hausse des prix des brebis et recul de la production de viande rouge entre 2010 et 2026

text:
Titre:
Forte hausse des prix des brebis et recul de la production de viande rouge entre 2010 et 2026


Date:
2026-07-06


Catégorie:
news


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://africanmanager.com/forte-hausse-des-prix-des-brebis-et-recul-de-la-production-de-viande-rouge-entre-2010-et-2026


Contenu:
. Les responsables du ministère ont également fait état d'un recul continu du cheptel national, particulièrement des femelles reproductrices, d'une baisse du nombre d'éleveurs et de difficultés d'accès au financement ayant conduit de nombreux exploitants à réduire leur activité ou à quitter le secteur. Cette situation a affecté la production nationale de viande rouge et de lait. Le ministère du Commerce souligne, pour sa part, que le déficit structurel de la production est accentué par la recrudescence de la contrebande et de l'abattage clandestin, limitant davantage l'offre sur le marché. Afin d'atténuer les tensions sur l'approvisionnement, les autorités ont procédé ces dernières années à des importations exceptionnelles de viandes réfrigérées et congelées, notamment durant les périodes de forte consommation. Face à ces défis, le ministère de l'Agriculture a présenté un programme de relance à l'horizon 2030 visant à reconstituer le cheptel national et à améliorer la productivité de la filière. Ce plan prévoit le soutien aux petits éleveurs, le développement de la production locale d'aliments pour bétail, le renforcement des services vétérinaires, l'amélioration génétique des races, la généralisation de l'identification électronique des animaux ainsi que la mise en place d'une plateforme numérique nationale de suivi du cheptel

metadata.title: Forte hausse des prix des brebis et recul de la production de viande rouge entre 2010 et 2026

Titre:
Le succès du Plan de développement 2026-2030 tributaire de la création de richesses


Date:
2026-07-04


Catégorie:
news


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://africanmanager.com/le-succes-du-plan-de-developpement-2026-2030-tributaire-de-la-creation-de-richesses


Contenu:
Titre:
Le succès du Plan de développement 2026-2030 tributaire de la création de richesses


Résumé:
Le député Saber Jelassi, président de la Commission de la planification stratégique et du développement durable, souligne que le financement reste le principal défi du Plan de développement 2026-2030 de la Tunisie. Environ 61 % des investissements (61,8 milliards de dinars sur 101 milliards) devraient être financés par le budget de l'État, ce qui soulève des inquiétudes compte tenu du déficit budgétaire. La commission appelle à une plus grande contribution des partenariats public-privé (actuellement seulement 8 %) et exprime des doutes sur la capacité des établissements publics à s'autofinancer à hauteur de 30 % (32 milliards de dinars), rappelant que le taux de réalisation du plan précédent n'a pas dépassé 40 %.

text:
Titre:
Le succès du Plan de développement 2026-2030 tributaire de la création de richesses


Date:
2026-07-04


Catégorie:
news


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://africanmanager.com/le-succes-du-plan-de-developpement-2026-2030-tributaire-de-la-creation-de-richesses


Contenu:
Le président de la Commission de la planification stratégique et du développement durable de l'Assemblée des représentants du peuple, le député Saber Jelassi, a confirmé que le financement constitue le principal point d'interrogation du Plan de développement 2026-2030, d'autant plus qu'environ 61 % des investissements totaux, soit 61,8 milliards de dinars sur 101 milliards, seront financés par le budget de l'État. Sur Express FM, il a expliqué que cette question avait fait l'objet de discussions approfondies au sein de la commission lors d'auditions avec le ministre de l'Économie et de la Planification et des représentants du ministère. Les députés se sont interrogés sur la capacité du budget de l'État à supporter un tel volume de financement, compte tenu des contraintes budgétaires et du déficit des finances publiques. Le parlementaire a souligné que la commission espérait une contribution plus importante des partenariats publics-privés, considérant que l'allocation actuelle de seulement 8 % pour ce type de financement est insuffisante et ne reflète pas le rôle attendu du secteur privé dans la réussite du plan. Il a ajouté que les expériences passées incitent à la prudence, rappelant que le taux de réalisation des projets du précédent plan de développement n'avait pas dépassé 40 %, ce qui rend les objectifs actuels plus proches de cibles que d'engagements réalisables

metadata.title: Le succès du Plan de développement 2026-2030 tributaire de la création de richesses

Il a ajouté que les expériences passées incitent à la prudence, rappelant que le taux de réalisation des projets du précédent plan de développement n'avait pas dépassé 40 %, ce qui rend les objectifs actuels plus proches de cibles que d'engagements réalisables. Le député a également exprimé des réserves quant au recours du plan à l'autofinancement par les institutions et établissements publics à hauteur d'environ 30 %, soit 32 milliards de dinars, s'interrogeant sur la capacité de ces institutions, dont la plupart sont confrontées à des difficultés financières, à mobiliser ces ressources.

Article:
Le dermatologue à l'hôpital des Forces de sécurité intérieure de La Marsa, Ismahan Souissi, a remporté le prix « ILDS Young Dermatologist International Achievement Award 2027 », décerné par La Ligue Internationale des Sociétés de Dermatologie (ILDS) pour la région Afrique. Cette distinction fait suite à une nomination présentée par la Société africaine de dermatologie et de vénérologie, qui a été approuvée par le conseil d'administration de la société internationale lors d'une réunion tenue le 20 juin 2026, après examen d'un nombre record de candidatures cette année. Souissi devrait recevoir ce prix lors de la cérémonie d'ouverture officielle du 26e Congrès mondial de dermatologie, qui se tiendra à Guadalajara, au Mexique, du 21 au 26 juin 2027. La direction de l'hôpital a exprimé sa fierté pour cette distinction internationale, qui illustre la compétence et l'excellence des cadres médicaux tunisiens sur la scène scientifique internationale. Ismahan Souissi est professeure de dermatologie à la Faculté de médecine de Tunis et a occupé le poste de secrétaire générale de la société tunisienne de dermatologie et de vénéréologie (STDV).

metadata.title: La dermatologue Ismahan Souissi remporte le prix « ILDS Young Dermatologist International Achievement Award 2027 »

text:
Titre:
Kenyan social commerce startup Twiva raises funding from Jobtech Alliance


Date:
2026-03-26


Catégorie:
funding


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/03/26/kenyan-social-commerce-startup-twiva-raises-funding-from-jobtech-alliance


Contenu:
Kenyan startup Twiva, which is transforming the opportunity landscape for African creators by replacing sporadic brand deals with structured demand, performance-based earnings, and integrated financial services, has raised an undisclosed amount of funding from the Jobtech Alliance. Disrupt Africa reported in October 2022 on the launch of the Jobtech Alliance, an ecosystem-building initiative around inclusive jobtech in Africa steered by Mercy Corps and BFA Global, which aims to create an enabling environment for entrepreneurs to build platforms that deliver quality livelihoods, are inclusive, and enable users to engage in decent work. It has run several cohorts, but is now also making direct investments in relevant ventures, most recently in Nigeria's Bumpa and Kenya's Flowcart, both e-commerce ventures. Jobtech Alliance has now backed Kenyan startup Twiva, which is tackling the "dual crisis" of youth unemployment and MSME barriers with its influencer-powered social commerce platform, which enables MSMEs to market and sell their products and services through online gig workers. The investment in Twiva, which took part in the fourth edition of the Visa Africa Fintech Accelerator last year, is undisclosed, but Jobtech Alliance has already been working with the startup for more than six months, providing bespoke venture support and engaging with creators on the platform

metadata.title: Kenyan social commerce startup Twiva raises funding from Jobtech Alliance

text:
Titre:
Kenyan social commerce startup Twiva raises funding from Jobtech Alliance


Date:
2026-03-26


Catégorie:
funding


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/03/26/kenyan-social-commerce-startup-twiva-raises-funding-from-jobtech-alliance


Contenu:
. The investment in Twiva, which took part in the fourth edition of the Visa Africa Fintech Accelerator last year, is undisclosed, but Jobtech Alliance has already been working with the startup for more than six months, providing bespoke venture support and engaging with creators on the platform. "In the process, we confirmed our hypothesis that building the infrastructure to generate consistent, repeat brand demand is the (rewarding!) challenge that platforms need to tackle," Jobtech Alliance said. Explaining the rationale behind the investment, Jobtech said it was clear that creator audiences are growing in Africa, with platforms multiplying and monetisation tools expanding. "But reliable incomes remain an issue. Even among top-tier creators, income stability is far from guaranteed. Earnings frequently fluctuate due to seasonal brand campaigns, platform algorithm changes, and shifts in audience engagement," it said. In addressing this, Twiva stood out to the organisation for two main reasons. "It deeply understands demand, and it deeply understands creators. The platform demonstrates that when you structure demand, embed payments, build skills, and treat creators as distributed businesses, you can build repeat revenue and customers for brands and more durable income for creators," Jobtech Alliance said. The majority of influencer marketplaces in Africa tend to have a singular focus on discovery. They aggregate talent and hope that demand follows

metadata.title: Kenyan social commerce startup Twiva raises funding from Jobtech Alliance

text:
Titre:
Kenyan social commerce startup Twiva raises funding from Jobtech Alliance


Date:
2026-03-26


Catégorie:
funding


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/03/26/kenyan-social-commerce-startup-twiva-raises-funding-from-jobtech-alliance


Contenu:
. The majority of influencer marketplaces in Africa tend to have a singular focus on discovery. They aggregate talent and hope that demand follows. Twiva does the opposite by structuring demand first. The platform connects brands and SMEs with micro and nano influencers, embedding campaign management, performance tracking, reporting, and structured payments directly into the workflow. Campaigns are increasingly tied to measurable outcomes, which means clicks, conversions, and sales, rather than just awareness. That is because, above all, brands care about ROI, accountability, and repeatable performance. But Twiva's strength is not just brand optimisation at the expense of creators, its demand structuring is in service of more predictable creator income - "the holy grail for many women users". "Women micro and nano creators cannot access brand work without digital infrastructure. They are not waiting for sporadic deals as they are excluded from agency rosters and formal campaign pipelines. Twiva lowers that barrier by embedding them into structured campaigns where income is tied to performance within clear workflows, with transparent metrics and an embedded payment system. Earnings for their creators become less arbitrary and more predictable," Jobtech Alliance said.

metadata.title: Kenyan social commerce startup Twiva raises funding from Jobtech Alliance

Contenu:
Titre:
African tech startup funding leaps by almost 50% as sector begins to recover from global "funding winter"


Résumé:
Total investment into African tech startups increased by nearly 50% to $1.64 billion in 2025, according to Disrupt Africa's 11th annual African Tech Startups Funding Report. 178 startups raised funds, a slight decline in deal count but a 46.2% increase in total capital. The 'big four' markets (Nigeria, Egypt, Kenya, South Africa) drove growth, while fintech remained the dominant sector. Active investors fell slightly to 330, stabilizing after years of decline.

text:
Titre:
African tech startup funding leaps by almost 50% as sector begins to recover from global "funding winter"


Date:
2026-02-03


Catégorie:
report


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/02/03/african-tech-startup-funding-leaps-by-almost-50-as-sector-begins-to-recover-from-global-funding-winter


Contenu:
Total investment into the African tech startup ecosystem increased by almost 50 per cent to US$1.64 billion in 2025 as the sector began to slowly recover from the impacts of the global capital shortage. This is according to the 11th edition of the annual African Tech Startups Funding Report released by startup news and research portal Disrupt Africa, which is available for free to all thanks to support from partners TVC Labs and Opus. A total of 178 startups raised a combined total of US$1.64 billion over the course of the year, which represented a slight decline in terms of the number of funded ventures, but an increase of 46.2 per cent on total raised funding. This comes after two years of decline, which saw startup funding fall to US$1,119,802,000 in 2024, as the sector bore the brunt of the global "funding winter". The number of active investors - individual or institutional - fell again in 2025, by 4.6 per cent to 330 from 346 in 2024. Yet this represented a stabilisation of sorts after two years of major decline. In 2022 there were 987 investors, and in 2023 there were 527. Each of the so-called "big four" of African tech - Nigeria, Egypt, Kenya and South Africa - raised more funding than in 2024, when steep declines occurred as a result of the global capital shortage

metadata.title: African tech startup funding leaps by almost 50% as sector begins to recover from global "funding winter"

text:
Titre:
African tech startup funding leaps by almost 50% as sector begins to recover from global "funding winter"


Date:
2026-02-03


Catégorie:
report


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/02/03/african-tech-startup-funding-leaps-by-almost-50-as-sector-begins-to-recover-from-global-funding-winter


Contenu:
. In 2022 there were 987 investors, and in 2023 there were 527. Each of the so-called "big four" of African tech - Nigeria, Egypt, Kenya and South Africa - raised more funding than in 2024, when steep declines occurred as a result of the global capital shortage. This meant they accounted for much of the continent's growth in 2025, as even though there are signs the "funding winter" is coming to an end, capital remained focused on markets perceived as being less "risky". Fintech, yet again, once again proved by far and away the most popular sector for investors in African tech startups in 2024, increasing its share of funded ventures and generally matching its proportion of total funding from 2024. The report is available for free download here. Aside from providing a full list of the funded startups, who invested in them, and, where possible, the amount raised, from the previous year, the annual reports also provide deep-dives into investment trends within key startup geographies and verticals, as well as data on African startup acquisitions. "2024 was a very difficult year indeed for African tech from a funding perspective, with a significant decline in investment for the second year in a row. Yet we've seen a big boost in 2025, and though funding levels have by no means "recovered", it seems like we are seeing light at the end of the tunnel from an investment perspective," said Disrupt Africa co-founder Gabriella Mulligan.

metadata.title: African tech startup funding leaps by almost 50% as sector begins to recover from global "funding winter"

text:
Titre:
Nigeria's Siiqo launches social commerce infrastructure on Telegram


Date:
2026-06-30


Catégorie:
startup


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/30/nigerias-siiqo-launches-social-commerce-infrastructure-on-telegram


Contenu:
Nigerian startup Siiqo launches commerce infrastructure on Telegram, helping users increase their sales and better manage their businesses. Founded by Okerere Innocent and Linda Kolapa Ogochukwu, Siiqo aims to help users build brands by creating online storefronts and portfolios, and getting discovered by a wider audience. It also allows them to get paid, via fiat or crypto, and manage business operations efficiently and effectively. The startup has now launched a full commerce infrastructure on Telegram to deepen social commerce in Nigeria. "We didn't set out to build on Telegram. Like most platforms in this space, our roadmap assumed the obvious next step after the web was a native mobile app. But the more time we spent with the SMEs and vendors actually using Siiqo, the clearer one pattern became - they weren't waiting for an app," said Innocent. "They were already running entire customer relationships - orders, prices, follow-ups, disputes - inside Telegram and WhatsApp threads, manually, with no system behind any of it." That observation is what pushed Siiqo to build a full commerce bot directly inside Telegram, instead of doubling down on app development. Vendors can now list products, take orders, and collect payment without ever leaving the chat interface their customers already trust

metadata.title: Nigeria's Siiqo launches social commerce infrastructure on Telegram

text:
Titre:
Nigeria's Siiqo launches social commerce infrastructure on Telegram


Date:
2026-06-30


Catégorie:
startup


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/30/nigerias-siiqo-launches-social-commerce-infrastructure-on-telegram


Contenu:
. Vendors can now list products, take orders, and collect payment without ever leaving the chat interface their customers already trust. "Critically, we built our escrow-based trust layer directly into that experience, so the same protection against fake payment alerts and scam transactions that defines Siiqo on the web now travels with vendors into Telegram," Innocent said. "We think this is a genuinely interesting strategic bet, not just a feature release: that the next phase of Nigerian social commerce isn't going to be won by better apps, but by infrastructure - trust, payments, verification - that lives natively inside the chat platforms where Nigerian SMEs are already operating."

metadata.title: Nigeria's Siiqo launches social commerce infrastructure on Telegram

Titre:
Experts internationaux à Hammamet pour la cybersécurité


Résumé:
La 17e édition d'AfricaCrypt 2026, conférence internationale annuelle dédiée à la cryptographie et à la cybersécurité, se tiendra à Hammamet du 8 au 10 juillet 2026. Organisée par l'International Association for Cryptologic Research (IACR), elle réunira chercheurs, experts et universitaires de plusieurs pays pour débattre des enjeux de la sécurité numérique, notamment l'informatique quantique et la cryptographie post-quantique. L'événement est co-organisé par l'Université de Tunis et le Centre tunisien de recherche militaire, avec le soutien de sociétés savantes tunisiennes et méditerranéennes. Une école d'été sur la cryptographie post-quantique précédera la conférence les 6 et 7 juillet pour jeunes chercheurs.

text:
Titre:
Experts internationaux à Hammamet pour la cybersécurité


Date:
2026-07-05


Catégorie:
event


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://africanmanager.com/africacrypt-2026-des-experts-internationaux-reunis-a-hammamet-autour-de-la-cybersecurite


Contenu:
La 17e édition d'AfricaCrypt 2026, la conférence internationale annuelle dédiée à la cryptographie et à la cybersécurité, se déroulera à Hammamet du 8 au 10 juillet. Organisé à l'initiative de l'International Association for Cryptologic Research (IACR), cet événement réunira des chercheurs, experts et universitaires venus de plusieurs pays pour débattre des enjeux actuels de la sécurité numérique. Cette nouvelle édition mettra en lumière les avancées scientifiques et technologiques les plus récentes dans les domaines de la cryptographie et de la cybersécurité. Les participants échangeront sur les défis posés par l'évolution rapide des technologies numériques, notamment l'émergence de l'informatique quantique, qui remet progressivement en question les systèmes cryptographiques traditionnels. Les discussions porteront, en outre, sur le développement de solutions innovantes destinées à protéger les infrastructures critiques et les données sensibles et sur la transition vers les nouvelles normes de cryptographie post-quantique. La conférence est organisée conjointement par l'Université de Tunis et le Centre tunisien de recherche militaire, avec le soutien de la Société tunisienne de mathématiques et de la Société méditerranéenne des sciences mathématiques, en collaboration avec les écoles doctorales des universités de Monastir et de Sousse

metadata.title: Experts internationaux à Hammamet pour la cybersécurité

Experts internationaux à Hammamet pour la cybersécurité

event

[]


Source:
https://africanmanager.com/africacrypt-2026-des-experts-internationaux-reunis-a-hammamet-autour-de-la-cybersecurite


Contenu:
. Elle réunira plusieurs spécialistes de renommée internationale, confirmant ainsi le rôle d'AfricaCrypt comme l'un des principaux rendez-vous scientifiques consacrés à la sécurité de l'information sur le continent africain. En prélude à la conférence, une école d'été dédiée à la cryptographie post-quantique se tiendra les 6 et 7 juillet. Destinée aux jeunes chercheurs d'Afrique et d'ailleurs, cette formation abordera les fondements de la cryptographie de nouvelle génération et présentera les principales familles d'algorithmes conçues pour résister aux futures capacités de calcul des ordinateurs quantiques. L'objectif est de préparer la relève scientifique aux défis de la cybersécurité de demain et d'encourager le développement de recherches innovantes dans ce domaine stratégique.

Titre:
Stade Tunisien : le gardien Atef Dkhili prolonge jusqu'en 2027


Résumé:
Le Stade Tunisien a annoncé la prolongation du contrat de son gardien Atef Dkhili jusqu'en 2027. Le club a également recruté Malek Chouikh (ailier gauche) jusqu'en 2028 et le milieu offensif sénégalais Mame Saliou Thioune jusqu'en 2029.


Date:
2026-07-03


Catégorie:
news


Secteurs:



Pays:
Tunisia


Startups mentionnées:



Investisseurs:



Fonds:



Article:
Le Stade Tunisien a annoncé, jeudi sur sa page officielle, la prolongation du contrat de son gardien Atef Dkhili jusqu'en 2027. Dkhili a notamment évolué au Club Africain et à l'Olympique de Béja. Il a également connu une expérience dans le championnat saoudien sous les couleurs d'Al-Aïn. Le club du Bardo avait auparavant annoncé le recrutement de Malek Chouikh, engagé jusqu'en 2028. Agé de 26 ans, Chouikh évolue au poste d'ailier gauche. Il a précédemment porté les couleurs de l'ES Radès, de l'Olympique de Béja et du club libyen d'Abu Salim. Il s'agit de la deuxième recrue du Stade Tunisien lors du mercato estival, après l'arrivée du milieu offensif sénégalais Mame Saliou Thioune, sous contrat jusqu'en 2029.

Contenu:
Nigerian startup Viewlocked is an AI-powered travel planning platform that helps travellers discover, organise, and eventually book every part of a trip in one place. Founded in 2025 by Abbey Ola, Viewlocked provides travel businesses with a platform to showcase their services to travellers who are actively planning trips, and helps travellers plan and pay for their trips. "Today, planning a trip often requires jumping between multiple websites and apps to find accommodations, experiences, transportation, guides, rentals, and local services. Viewlocked brings these elements together into a single platform, allowing travellers to plan more efficiently while discovering trusted local businesses and unique experiences," Ola said. The idea emerged from observing how fragmented travel planning remains despite advances in travel technology. Viewlocked aims to unify those experiences into a single planning environment while helping travellers discover local businesses that may otherwise be difficult to find. The bootstrapped Viewlocked, which recently began conversations around a seed funding round to support product development, market expansion, and growth initiatives, has built a global network of travel partners across multiple destinations before launch, entirely through founder-led outreach and without paid marketing

text:
Titre:
Village Capital distributes $500k grants to 32 climate startups


Date:
2026-06-29


Catégorie:
funding


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/29/village-capital-distributes-500k-grants-to-32-climate-startups


Contenu:
Village Capital has announced the conclusion of the Empowering Sustainable Entrepreneurship Africa (ESEA), a three-year initiative established to strengthen locally-led entrepreneurial ecosystems and expand pathways to capital for climate startups across Africa. Village Capital has partnered Norad - the Norwegian Agency for Development Cooperation to run ESEA, which was designed around a core belief that entrepreneurs do not scale in isolation. Through ESEA, Village Capital worked with 14 locally-led ecosystem builders across Ghana, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, and Tanzania to strengthen their organisations and test a co-delivery model for founder support. Building on this foundation, it partnered with five ecosystem builders in 2025 to co-deliver investment-readiness programmes for 150 climate startups across the five markets. As the initiative concludes, Village Capital has deployed US$500,000 in grant funding to 32 startups addressing critical sustainability challenges across the continent. The largest share of funding was directed toward climate adaptation startups, which received US$225,000. Food security startups received US$125,000, blue economy startups received US$100,000, and renewable energy startups received US$50,000. "At Village Capital, we've learned that the challenge isn't a lack of entrepreneurial talent

metadata.title: Village Capital distributes $500k grants to 32 climate startups

text:
Titre:
Village Capital distributes $500k grants to 32 climate startups


Date:
2026-06-29


Catégorie:
funding


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/29/village-capital-distributes-500k-grants-to-32-climate-startups


Contenu:
. Food security startups received US$125,000, blue economy startups received US$100,000, and renewable energy startups received US$50,000. "At Village Capital, we've learned that the challenge isn't a lack of entrepreneurial talent. Too often, the challenge is that the systems around entrepreneurs are not designed to help promising startups access the networks, resources, and capital they need to grow," said Nakami Walunywa, regional director for Africa at Village Capital. "ESEA gave us an opportunity to test what happens when you work through local organisations that are deeply embedded in their markets. We saw how local knowledge, trusted founder relationships, and ecosystem intelligence can help surface stronger pipelines and create more effective pathways between entrepreneurs and capital. Those lessons are shaping how we think about ecosystem building, not as a parallel effort to investing, but as essential infrastructure for directing capital where it can create the greatest long-term value."

metadata.title: Village Capital distributes $500k grants to 32 climate startups

South African gym management platform Octiv, which is powering 1,400 gyms and more than 200,000 active users across over 40 countries, has launched Octiv Explore, a new consumer-facing feature that turns the platform into a discovery engine for the boutique and independent fitness sector. Octiv is a global fitness business management platform with tools for scheduling, membership management, payments, access control and, now, consumer discovery. It empowers gym owners to run stronger, smarter and more scalable businesses. The release of Octiv Explore is Octiv's biggest to date and accelerates the company's push to become a global category leader in fitness technology. Octiv Explore acts as a digital storefront for every gym on the platform, placing facilities on an interactive map where members can search, view profiles, book classes and pay, all in one seamless flow. For independent gyms, that means new acquisition channels with zero additional admin. For consumers, it means finding the right gym in seconds, whether at home or travelling. "Octiv Explore is built to bring gyms more traffic, more conversions and more opportunities, without adding more to their workload," said Mark Fawzy, founder and CEO of Octiv. "Independent gyms are the heart of the global fitness landscape

The Jobtech Alliance, a partnership between Mercy Corps and BFA Global, has launched two programmes - the Catalyst Jobtech Accelerator and the Jobtech Innovation Lab - to support early-stage digital platforms connecting people to work opportunities across Africa. The accelerator will host two cohorts of four innovators each, targeting startups operating in Kenya, Uganda, Nigeria, Senegal, Ethiopia, Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, Sudan, and South Sudan. text:
Titre:
Jobtech Alliance launches startup accelerator programmes to improve livelihoods in Africa


Date:
2022-10-31


Catégorie:
event


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2022/10/31/jobtech-alliance-launches-startup-accelerator-programmes-to-improve-livelihoods-in-africa


Contenu:
The Jobtech Alliance, an ecosystem-building initiative around inclusive jobtech in Africa steered by Mercy Corps and BFA Global, has launched two programmes - the Catalyst Jobtech Accelerator and the Jobtech Innovation Lab - designed to support digital platforms that connect people to work opportunities that build livelihoods. A partnership between Mercy Corps and BFA Global, the Jobtech Alliance aims to create an enabling environment for entrepreneurs to build platforms that deliver quality livelihoods, are inclusive, and enable users to engage in decent work. Building on the experience of BFA Global's Catalyst Fund model, the Catalyst Job Accelerator will host two cohorts of four early-stage innovators creating affordable, accessible, and appropriate jobtech solutions that connect young people to jobs. The Catalyst Job Accelerator will focus on innovations with the potential to scale and meaningfully increase jobs and income, and grant resources that would substantially impact the team's ability to test and improve their product. The target market or operations of the startup should be in Kenya, Uganda, Nigeria, Senegal, Ethiopia, Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, Sudan, or South Sudan. "By building an ecosystem where entrepreneurs have access to knowledge and networks that can help scale their businesses, we're starting to change the conversation about the future of work and what can be achieved," said Michelle Hassan, Kenya country manager at BFA Global

metadata.title: Jobtech Alliance launches startup accelerator programmes to improve livelihoods in Africa

text:
Titre:
Jobtech Alliance launches startup accelerator programmes to improve livelihoods in Africa


Date:
2022-10-31


Catégorie:
event


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2022/10/31/jobtech-alliance-launches-startup-accelerator-programmes-to-improve-livelihoods-in-africa


Contenu:
. "By building an ecosystem where entrepreneurs have access to knowledge and networks that can help scale their businesses, we're starting to change the conversation about the future of work and what can be achieved," said Michelle Hassan, Kenya country manager at BFA Global. The Jobtech Innovation Lab partners with jobtech platforms on individual sprints which have the potential to enhance scale, impact, or inclusivity of platforms with their users. This could include developing new products or services; enhancing products, operations, or marketing in order to better deliver services, reach new markets, or remove barriers to access for marginalised populations; and supporting market expansion, particularly towards underserved markets. "There are many great opportunities for jobtech platforms to increase their scale, impact, and inclusivity on the continent, but there is a lack of time, capital, and capacity to deliver on them. We're keen to work with these platforms to further their business goals and improve outcomes for their platform users," said Chris Maclay, Mercy Corps Jobtech Alliance programme director. Each of the selected companies will receive capital, bespoke venture-building support and have the opportunity to work closely with the BFA Global and Mercy Corps teams, industry experts, mentors, and ecosystem partners to scale their impact. To apply, interested parties can visit the Catalyst Jobtech Accelerator and Jobtech Innovation Lab websites.

metadata.title: Jobtech Alliance launches startup accelerator programmes to improve livelihoods in Africa

funding

Article:
South Africa's Kloset Klub, a fast-growing circular fashion platform, has secured a round of seed funding to help it expand operations. Founded by Phumi Körber, Kloset Klub helps people buy and sell quality pre-loved clothing through curated resale, peer-to-peer shopping, and managed closet services. The platform helps sellers unlock value from their wardrobes while giving buyers access to premium pre-loved fashion at more accessible prices. The startup has now raised an undisclosed amount of seed funding from Thinkroom, an African entrepreneurship and innovation firm, and co-investor Frank Smit. "This investment is more than just capital, it's a vote of confidence in the journey we've been on," said Körber. "Building a business is never linear, and having partners like Thinkroom and Frank, who understand that reality, makes a huge difference. We're excited to keep building something that not only looks good, but does good too." "We've watched Phumi build Kloset Klub through real challenges, real pivots, requiring real grit," said Catherine Young, founder of Thinkroom. "Phumi's resilience and her approach to building a business stood out early on. We are incredibly proud to be writing the first cheque into Kloset Klub and to support Phumi as she scales."

metadata.title: SA circular fashion startup Kloset Klub raises seed funding

La Kia EV4 remporte la distinction « Best of the Best », soulignant l'innovation du design Kia. La Kia PV5 décroche le prix « Winner », mettant en avant un design PBV flexible et centré sur l'utilisateur. Cette reconnaissance confirme le leadership mondial de Kia en matière de design et la force de sa philosophie « Opposites United ». La plateforme robotique mobile MobED du groupe constructeur reçoit également le prix « Winner », illustrant un design innovant et une grande praticité d'utilisation. Kia Corporation a remporté les plus hautes distinctions lors des Red Dot Award: Product Design 2026. La Kia EV4 a obtenu la récompense suprême « Best of the Best », tandis que la Kia PV5 a été distinguée par le prix « Winner ». De son côté, la plateforme robotique mobile MobED du groupe constructeur a également été récompensée dans la catégorie « Winner ». Cette reconnaissance renforce le leadership mondial de Kia en matière de design et reflète l'impact continu de sa philosophie « Opposites United », à travers des solutions de mobilité innovantes, durables et centrées sur l'utilisateur. « C'est un honneur pour Kia d'être une nouvelle fois récompensée par les Red Dot Design Award, en particulier avec l'EV4 qui décroche la plus haute distinction », a déclaré Karim Habib, Vice-Président Exécutif et Directeur du Design Global de Kia

. « C'est un honneur pour Kia d'être une nouvelle fois récompensée par les Red Dot Design Award, en particulier avec l'EV4 qui décroche la plus haute distinction », a déclaré Karim Habib, Vice-Président Exécutif et Directeur du Design Global de Kia. « Ces récompenses reflètent l'impact de notre philosophie "Opposites United" dans la conception de solutions de mobilité pertinentes et porteuses de sens, offrant des expériences intuitives et fluides à nos utilisateurs. De la réinterprétation audacieuse de la berline avec l'EV4 à l'approche flexible et centrée sur l'utilisateur de la PV5 dans l'univers PBV, ces deux modèles illustrent parfaitement l'engagement de Kia à associer émotion et praticité au quotidien. » Pourquoi la Kia EV4 a-t-elle été sacrée « Best of the Best » ? La Kia EV4 a été récompensée pour son approche audacieuse et innovante du design de la berline, introduisant une nouvelle vision dans ce segment traditionnel. Développée selon la philosophie « Opposites United » de Kia, l'EV4 se distingue par une silhouette fastback marquante qui bouscule les codes établis. La version hatchback renforce davantage sa polyvalence en associant des proportions compactes et dynamiques à une grande praticité, spécialement pensée pour répondre aux attentes des clients européens

. La version hatchback renforce davantage sa polyvalence en associant des proportions compactes et dynamiques à une grande praticité, spécialement pensée pour répondre aux attentes des clients européens. Comment la Kia PV5 illustre-t-elle la stratégie PBV de Kia ? Lauréate du prix « Winner », la Kia PV5 met en avant la stratégie visionnaire Platform Beyond Vehicle (PBV) de Kia ainsi que son approche modulaire du design. Elle adopte une face avant commune associée à des modules arrière flexibles pouvant être adaptés à différents usages. La gamme comprend des versions Passenger, Cargo, WAV (Wheelchair Accessible Vehicle) et Crew, chacune conçue pour offrir un espace optimisé, une grande praticité et une excellente fonctionnalité, tout en conservant une identité stylistique cohérente. Cette nouvelle distinction vient enrichir le palmarès grandissant du PV5, après l'obtention récente d'un Gold Award lors des iF Design Awards 2026. Le succès continu de Kia aux Red Dot Award Les performances constantes de Kia dans les grandes compétitions internationales de design témoignent de la solidité de sa stratégie design à long terme. Les modèles Kia ayant remporté le titre « Best of the Best » aux Red Dot Award sont désormais : EV6 (2022), EV9 (2024), EV3 (2025), EV4 (2026)

Qu'est-ce qui fait du MobED de HMG un lauréat des Red Dot Award ? Développé par le Robotics LAB du groupe constructeur, MobED (Mobile Eccentric Droid) est une plateforme robotique mobile conçue pour évoluer sur différents terrains et dans de multiples environnements d'utilisation. MobED incarne la philosophie de design « Refined Edge » du Robotics LAB, une approche visant à améliorer continuellement l'intégration des robots dans le quotidien humain. Cette identité stylistique façonne non seulement son apparence distinctive, mais également ses interactions, afin d'inspirer confiance et proximité dans des espaces partagés.

Article:
South Africa's Nedbank has announced the winners of the Nedbank N*ovation Data and Analytics Masters Challenge 2026, following weeks of rigorous virtual competition and an in-person finale in Johannesburg. The national Data and Analytics Challenge is designed to identify and recruit South Africa's most advanced data and analytics practitioners, and this year's edition culminated in a live contest where finalists were tested on their ability to solve complex, real-world banking problems. The winner of the data engineering track was Mogale Thobejane, with Tshifhiwa Gift Moila and Tinashe Chinyati coming in second and third place. The data science and machine learning track was won by Edward Rycroft, with Alex Jacobs in second and Vusani Khosa in third. Contestants shared ZAR500,000 (US$30,000) in prize money. The top performers will benefit from a fast-tracked recruitment process, highlighting the bank's ongoing investment in building future-ready data and analytics capabilities. In collaboration with partners including OfferZen, Zindi, Otinga, and Microsoft, the challenge potentially connects top talent directly with career opportunities. "This challenge is about more than competition, it reflects our belief that data excellence is a strategic capability, and this challenge was a novel one with a positive outcome demonstrated by the level of quality on display at the challenge," said Nedbank Group technology executive Thando Lukhele.

metadata.title: Winners of Nedbank N*ovation Data and Analytics Masters Challenge announced

Article:
African startups are invited to attend ClawCon Nairobi, a free, community-led gathering focused on personal AI, agents, workflows, OpenClaw, and the emerging tools that let individuals build more powerful digital systems for themselves. Set for June 18 at Strathmore University, ClawCon is designed for builders, founders, developers, creators, operators, researchers, students, and AI-curious people. It will feature live demos, founder presentations, Q&As, and conversations around what personal AI could mean for Nairobi's tech ecosystem. The event was born from the OpenClaw community, and its mission to put power back into people's hands - giving individuals more sovereignty over personal AI, their workflows, their tools, and their digital lives.

text:
Titre:
Kenya's Fikra launches AI inference API for African developers, startups, businesses


Date:
2026-06-30


Catégorie:
startup


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/30/kenyas-fikra-launches-ai-inference-api-for-african-developers-startups-businesses


Contenu:
Kenya's Fikra API has launched an AI inference API built specifically for African developers, startups, and businesses. Founded by James Miano earlier this year, Fikra API is an OpenAI-compatible inference API targeting African developers, with M-Pesa payments built in and pricing in local terms rather than USD. Developers can send requests to Fikra's endpoint and get responses from large language models, the same way they would with OpenAI or Anthropic, but with pricing, payment methods, and token economics designed for African markets. "If you are a developer or startup founder in Nairobi, Lagos, or Cape Town and you want to build an AI-powered product, the standard path is OpenAI or Anthropic. Both require a minimum spend of around US$20 before you can access the API," Miano told Disrupt Africa. "That figure sounds small, but in Nairobi US$20 is close to a week's salary for many people. For those who can afford it, the problem does not stop there, a frontier model output pricing is expensive and burns through tokens quickly, forcing constant top-ups that compound the cost." The second barrier is payment. "Most people in Africa are not cardholders. OpenAI and Anthropic require a credit or debit card. Mobile money is how the majority of people here actually transact, and none of the major AI API providers support it," Miano said. Fikra API exists to solve both. Fully-bootstrapped, the startup onboarded its first 10 users within days of launch

metadata.title: Kenya's Fikra launches AI inference API for African developers, startups, businesses

text:
Titre:
Kenya's Fikra launches AI inference API for African developers, startups, businesses

Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/30/kenyas-fikra-launches-ai-inference-api-for-african-developers-startups-businesses


Contenu:
. OpenAI and Anthropic require a credit or debit card. Mobile money is how the majority of people here actually transact, and none of the major AI API providers support it," Miano said. Fikra API exists to solve both. Fully-bootstrapped, the startup onboarded its first 10 users within days of launch. "We have also shipped two proprietary models - a custom embeddings model ready for inference, and Fikra Nano 1B which is a ternary weight fine-tuned model we built in-house. The ternary weight pipeline is a genuinely uncommon capability and gives us a technical foundation most API providers at this stage do not have," said Miano. Currently only active in Kenya, with payment infrastructure live via Paystack which covers much of sub-Saharan Africa, Fikra's model is built to scale continent-wide. "Any African developer with a smartphone and mobile money access is the target user," said Miano. The startup charges US$1 per two million tokens. "We do not split input and output pricing the way most providers do, it is one flat rate. Payment is via Paystack, which supports M-Pesa and mobile money across multiple African markets. We are pre-revenue at this stage with the commercial push beginning at launch," said Miano. "The main challenge is onboarding friction, getting a developer from signup to their first successful API call as fast as possible, because if that moment takes too long you lose them. We identified this early and have been iterating on it

https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/12/holocene-closes-southern-africas-first-dedicated-climate-tech-fund


Contenu:
VC firm Holocene has announced the final close of Holocene Ventures Fund I (HVF1), Southern Africa's first dedicated high-growth climate tech fund. Holocene targets promising local founders at the earliest stages who lack capital and hands-on operational support to scale, especially in a nascent sector such as climate-tech. Through HVF1, it has backed 10 companies in the 18 months it has been deployed, creating over 500 jobs and achieving a 2x markup on invested capital, while attracting US$8 in follow-on capital for every US$1 it invested. "It's clear we need to dramatically accelerate the pace of climate innovation in Africa," founder Josh Romisher said. "The continent will double in size, urbanise, and begin to truly consume during our lifetimes. That can be seen as an impending climate catastrophe or a massive innovation opportunity. We choose to view it as the latter." Portfolio ventures include FARO, a circular economy company built around technology-enabled supply chains for unused inventory; ScootHero, which is deploying over 500 electric motorbikes and more than 50 battery-swap stations across South Africa; and Yongeza, an e-mobility infrastructure company in Uganda. Each investment comes with at least 12 months of embedded, high-touch support from a team of globally trained specialists: operators, executives-in-residence, and experts in revenue growth, fundraising, and grant writing

metadata.title: Holocene closes Southern Africa's first dedicated climate-tech fund

Contenu:
. Each investment comes with at least 12 months of embedded, high-touch support from a team of globally trained specialists: operators, executives-in-residence, and experts in revenue growth, fundraising, and grant writing. "What excites me most is not just that Holocene backed us early, it's that they got in the trenches with us," said Wahlied Cole, founder and CEO of ScootHero. "When we needed help with go-to-market, they brought in operators. When we were hitting a ceiling on capital, they helped us unlock grants and asset finance. It wasn't just capital. It was a partnership built on real understanding of what African founders need." Jonathan Smit, exited founder and HVF1 investor, said he invested in Holocene because he believes climate innovation will be one of the defining challenges and opportunities of our generation, particularly in Africa. "What stood out to me was not just the investment thesis, but the quality of the team and the hands-on way they support founders. Holocene is helping build the ecosystem and companies that can deliver meaningful climate impact while creating lasting economic value," he said. The final close marks the beginning of the fund's value-creation phase - the work of driving portfolio companies towards scale and exits. "We've shown that the intersection of climate impact and financial returns is real," Romisher said. "The task now is to scale that proof. We live the Stanford motto: change lives, change organisations, and change the world

text:
Titre:
How Nigeria's Sokofa connects diaspora buyers with African artisans, small vendors


Date:
2026-07-06


Catégorie:
startup


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/07/06/how-nigerias-sokofa-connects-diaspora-buyers-with-african-artisans-small-vendors


Contenu:
Nigerian startup Sokofa is a marketplace connecting diaspora buyers across Europe and North America with verified African artisans and small vendors across fashion, art, and beauty. Founded late last year, Sokofa provides access to fashion listings that let a buyer enter their own measurements directly on the product page, up to 16 specific fields depending on the garment, and have the piece cut by hand by the actual vendor, not a factory running standard sizes. The same listings carry an AI virtual try-on tool, where a buyer uploads a photo and gets a generated preview of the outfit on them in under 25 seconds, with the photo deleted after 24 hours. "The vendors are independent artisans currently based in Africa, selling Ankara sets, Adire chords, kaftans, and traditional native wear, verified by Sokofa before they are allowed to list. It is a small, real example of an African startup solving the fit and trust problem that diaspora shoppers have dealt with for years, using a mix of straightforward custom order infrastructure and AI rather than either alone," founder Tope Abuloye told Disrupt Africa. At its core, Abuloye said Sokofa is about putting Africa on the map in global e-commerce "on its own terms". "Diaspora buyers wanting authentic African fashion, art, and beauty have had two weak options: generic marketplaces with no fit guarantee or vendor verification, or relying on word of mouth tailors back home with long lead times and no buyer protection," said Abuloye

metadata.title: How Nigeria's Sokofa connects diaspora buyers with African artisans, small vendors

https://disruptafrica.com/2026/07/06/how-nigerias-sokofa-connects-diaspora-buyers-with-african-artisans-small-vendors


Contenu:
. "Diaspora buyers wanting authentic African fashion, art, and beauty have had two weak options: generic marketplaces with no fit guarantee or vendor verification, or relying on word of mouth tailors back home with long lead times and no buyer protection," said Abuloye. "We wanted African artisans to be discoverable and trusted by a global audience the same way any other category of maker is, not treated as a niche or an afterthought. Sokofa brings vendor verification, made to measure ordering, and AI fit preview into one checkout flow, a combination we haven't seen elsewhere in this category." Sokofa is self-funded, built on personal savings and support from family and friends, and has already listed multiple vendors, and made a number of sales. Vendors are based across Africa, while buyers are across Europe and North America. "We're actively expanding vendor recruitment to include Africans in the diaspora who want to sell African-inspired products themselves, several have already expressed interest in listing on the platform even while based outside the continent," Abuloye said. Sokofa runs on a commission model, taking a percentage of each sale.

metadata.title: How Nigeria's Sokofa connects diaspora buyers with African artisans, small vendors

Contenu:
Titre:
African Payment Providers Bypass SWIFT for Stablecoin Settlement


Résumé:
African payment service providers (PSPs) are increasingly adopting stablecoin settlement via USDC and USDT to bypass SWIFT's slow, costly correspondent banking system. Swiss-based institutional OTC desk FinchTrade facilitates this shift, offering same-day settlement, reduced FX exposure, and predictable fees. The article highlights a $1M Nigeria-to-Germany payment costing $5,000-$8,000 via stablecoins versus $40,000-$70,000 via SWIFT, with settlement compressed from days to hours. Regulatory frameworks in Nigeria and South Africa are evolving to support this infrastructure, with expansion expected to more African corridors.

text:
Titre:
African Payment Providers Bypass SWIFT for Stablecoin Settlement


Date:
2026-06-28


Catégorie:
news


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/28/african-payment-providers-bypass-swift-for-stablecoin-settlement


Contenu:
For African PSPs and EMIs handling cross-border B2B settlement, correspondent banking has become a structural drag. SWIFT settlement windows run T+3 to T+5. Cumulative fees hit 3% to 7% across intermediary banks. Working capital sits tied up in days-long FX exposure. These are now line items PSPs measure against alternatives that didn't exist 18 months ago. FinchTrade, a Swiss-based institutional OTC desk, sees this shift daily. The company serves African payment companies as well as other international flows where stablecoin settlement through USDC and USDT has become an operational default. "For our PSP clients, this is no longer about offering crypto products to end users," says Nicola Boldrini, growth lead at FinchTrade. "It's about whether their cross-border settlement actually settles the same day or takes a week." Three operational realities are driving the migration: settlement reliability, FX exposure compression, and cost predictability. A Nigerian importer paying a German supplier $1 million faces compounding differences: SWIFT settlement typically costs $40,000 to $70,000 with four to five days of FX volatility, while the same payment routed through Naira-to-USDC-to-Euro clears within hours at $5,000 to $8,000 total cost. FinchTrade provides the crypto-fiat conversion layer and connects African PSPs to global stablecoin liquidity, using regulated stablecoin issuers, institutional custody (Fireblocks), and compliance platforms

metadata.title: African Payment Providers Bypass SWIFT for Stablecoin Settlement

text:
Titre:
African Payment Providers Bypass SWIFT for Stablecoin Settlement


Date:
2026-06-28


Catégorie:
news


Secteurs:
[]


Source:
https://disruptafrica.com/2026/06/28/african-payment-providers-bypass-swift-for-stablecoin-settlement


Contenu:
. FinchTrade provides the crypto-fiat conversion layer and connects African PSPs to global stablecoin liquidity, using regulated stablecoin issuers, institutional custody (Fireblocks), and compliance platforms. FinchTrade holds a Swiss license through VQF and has processed over $1 billion in client trading volume since launch, running corridor settlement across Nigerian naira, Ghanaian cedi, Kenyan shilling, and South African rand. The business impact includes settlement time compressed from days to hours, all-in costs reduced by an order of magnitude, and working capital released from in-flight FX exposure. The shift also opens corridors that correspondent banking has historically underserved. Regulatory frameworks are maturing: the Central Bank of Nigeria has indicated openness to stablecoin payment infrastructure in B2B contexts, and South Africa's SARB framework provides workable guidelines. Additional corridors positioned to follow include Tanzania, Uganda, Senegal, and Côte d'Ivoire. Risks remain, including stablecoin issuer concentration.

metadata.title: African Payment Providers Bypass SWIFT for Stablecoin Settlement

Egypt's BrainsMingle, an AI-driven, video-first professional network designed to prioritise authentic human connection, has raised US$400,000 in seed funding. Founded by Belal Amin and Yousef Gamal, BrainsMingle allows experts to deliver live sessions, build thriving communities, manage bookings, and earn from their expertise while ambitious professionals discover world-class knowledge, engage with experts across the globe, and connect with peers who will shape their careers. BrainsMingle, which has already attracted professionals and communities from more than 90 countries, has announced a US$400,000 strategic seed investment from BasharSoft Group, Egypt's leading human capital technology group, which operates WUZZUF and Forasna. This partnership represents BasharSoft's first strategic investment since its acquisition of iCareer last year. BasharSoft Group, which serves over 9 million users through its flagship platforms WUZZUF and Forasna, as well as iCareer and Recruitera, is betting on BrainsMingle to lead the next generation of professional engagement and knowledge-sharing technologies. "We built BrainsMingle around one belief: the most valuable professional moments happen between people, not between people and content. The world does not need another feed to scroll or another profile to maintain. It needs a home, a single place where an ambitious professional can show up, share what they know, and build a community around it," Amin said

Titre:

Date:

Catégorie:
news

Contenu:
NEW YORK (Etats-Unis), 30 juin 2026 (TAP) - Une chaleur extrême attendra les supporters et les joueurs cette semaine lors de la Coupe du monde, alors qu'un « dôme de chaleur » s'installe sur le centre et l'est des États-Unis - ainsi que sur certaines régions du Canada - en parallèle au lancement de la phase à élimination directe du tournoi. Ce phénomène météorologique - une vaste zone de haute pression qui retient la chaleur et l'humidité - pourrait provoquer des températures dangereusement élevées dans certaines régions du Midwest et de la côte Est américaine. De telles conditions devraient perdurer tout au long du week-end prochain, de Toronto à Kansas City en passant par East Rutherford (New Jersey) et Philadelphie. « Même après le coucher du soleil, il fera encore très chaud », a déclaré Alan Reppert, météorologue chez AccuWeather. « Nous sommes dans une configuration qui va vraiment apporter de la chaleur pendant une bonne partie de l'après-midi et même jusque dans la soirée. » A New York, où un huitième de finale est prévu le 5 juillet, des températures à un pic inédit depuis 2013 sont attendus, a dit Reppert, précisant que même les matchs disputés en soirée pourraient être affectés. « Le soleil sera couché. Cela contribuera à améliorer la situation, mais il fera tout de même très chaud

metadata.title: Mondial-2026 : chaleur extrême attendue cette semaine pour joueurs et supporters

Titre:

[]


Source:
https://africanmanager.com/mondial-2026-chaleur-extreme-attendue-cette-semaine-pour-joueurs-et-supporters


Contenu:
. « Le soleil sera couché. Cela contribuera à améliorer la situation, mais il fera tout de même très chaud. » Ces conditions pourraient raviver les débats sur la sécurité et le bien-être des joueurs, déjà soulevé il y a un an lors de la Coupe du monde des clubs organisée aux Etats-Unis, le syndicat mondial des joueurs déclarant à l'époque que la canicule constatée devait servir de « signal d'alarme ». La FIFA a instauré pour cette Coupe du monde 2026 des pauses d'hydratation obligatoires de trois minutes lors de chaque mi-temps, une mesure jugée par ses partisans comme bénéfique pour le bien-être des joueurs mais dont les détracteurs estiment qu'elle perturbe le déroulement du jeu. L'instance dirigeante du football mondial n'a pas répondu dans l'immédiat à une demande de commentaire concernant d'éventuelles mesures supplémentaires qui pourraient être prises pour assurer la sécurité des joueurs ou des supporters pendant la période de forte chaleur prévue. Trois des stades accueillant des matchs cette semaine - Atlanta, Dallas et Houston - sont équipés de toits rétractables et de climatisation, ce qui apporte un soulagement considérable. Mais même une simple promenade jusqu'au stade ultramoderne des Dallas Cowboys peut s'apparenter à marcher sur du feu, alors que la ville texane, avec ses vastes étendues de béton et d'asphalte, a été conçue pour les voitures et est particulièrement propice à l'accumulation de chaleur.

Cascador, an Africa-focused platform for growth-stage founders building businesses with social impact, has deployed over US$5 million into seven innovative startups at its second annual pitch day. Disrupt Africa reported last March that Cascador had announced the launch of its Catalytic Fund, a US$2 million annual initiative providing tailored financial support to Cascador alumni companies. Since its founding in 2019, Cascador has supported over 60 entrepreneurs who have collectively raised more than US$55 million in capital. The company hosted its second annual on June 3, where over US$5 million of growth capital was deployed to seven innovative African entrepreneurs, who pitched their vision and growth strategies before an influential audience of business leaders and a distinguished panel of judges. The funding recipients included Agriarche, which secured US$1.7 million in debt funding; Koolboks, which banked US$1.4 million in debt; Powerstove, which secured debt funding worth US$1.2 million; and First Electric, which raised US$357,000 in debt. The other funded ventures were Fortics, which raised US$142,000 in debt; Stears, which banked US$450,000 in equity funding; and Indigenius AI, which raised US$250,000 in equity investment. "In just two years, Pitch Day has awarded more than US$9 million to growth-stage African founders, helping to build a new generation of entrepreneurs equipped to scale transformative businesses
